questions.question: What classical algorithm can be utilized to safely avoid deadlocks in resource allocation?
Which of the following is NOT one of the Coffman conditions required for a deadlock?
What is a binary semaphore commonly called when used strictly as a locking mechanism?
What condition occurs when multiple threads modify shared data concurrently with output depending on execution order?
What state does a system enter when it spends more time swapping pages than executing instructions?
What is a page fault?
answers: An interrupt triggered when a program accesses a page not currently mapped in RAM
A physical hardware failure inside RAM modules
A data anomaly where two files share the same sector
A crash caused by syntax compile errors

questions.question: What is the main difference between internal and external fragmentation?
answers: Internal is fixed by defragmentation; external requires virtual memory flushes
Internal occurs inside fixed allocated blocks; external occurs between variable blocks
External happens inside kernel space; internal happens in user space
External happens on hard drives; internal happens inside CPU registers

questions.question: What is the role of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
answers: To store execution metrics logs for background tasks
To map file allocation records sequentially on disk
To handle network data package buffering streams
To act as a specialized cache for virtual-to-physical address translations

questions.question: What state does a process enter if it is waiting for an I/O operation to finish?
What differentiates a process from a thread?
answers: Threads run in kernel mode; processes run in user mode
A single thread can contain multiple independent processes
Processes share file descriptors by default; threads do not
A process has its own isolated address space; threads share address spaces
